feedback.text: I'd very much appreciate it if there could be some attention towards ownership of products and the deceptive methods involving that. Specifically purchasing video games and your right for ownership. If you sell video games as an one time purchase then by all means you should now own that instance of the product you've bought. You're not renting. It is incredibly deceptive to make such purchases count as a license. My issue lies with the deception of such purchases. I'm not against the ability of renting or using licenses, but I do strongly believe that when you make purchases like that it should be very clear and upfront about the nature of your purchase.
